observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Establish RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year using
the duly certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and
collectible based on the RPTAR/Taxpayer's Index Cards
submitted by the Municipal Treasurer; and
b. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the understatement in
the RPT/SET Receivables to reflect the correct balances as of
year-end.

2.13 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Begin the recognition of an allowance for impairment on
receivables deemed uncollectible to ensure fair presentation while
awaiting write-off approval;
b. Initiate, through the Municipal Mayor, the filing of a request for
write-off/ derecognition from the books of dormant receivables
accounts, and undertake the necessary actions in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2023-008, should an extension be granted for its
implementation;
c. Strengthen internal control system to prevent the recurrence of long-
outstanding and undocumented receivables in the future.

3.7 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the appropriate
adjusting entry to correct the overstatement of inventory
amounting to ₱7,751.00 in the FS; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Acting General Services
Officer to conduct periodic reconciliation of inventory records
with the physical count. Any discrepancies should be promptly
investigated and adjusted to ensure accurate and fair presentation
of inventory accounts.

3.8 The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM)
No. 2025-009 dated April 2, 2025 for the foregoing observations and
recommendations.

4.9 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all future
purchases of semi-expendable properties are initially recorded as
inventories and only expensed upon issuance to the end-user and
supported with ICS to monitor accountability, in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2024-006;
b. Require the Acting General Services Officer and the Municipal
Accountant maintain the SPC and the SPLC for each class of semi-
expendable property, respectively; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to strengthen internal controls to
ensure that semi-expendable properties acquired are properly
evaluated and recorded in accordance with COA Circular
No. 2024-006.

4.10 The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM)
No. 2025-011 dated April 11, 2025 for the foregoing observations and
recommendations.

5.13 We recommended and the Management agreed to require the
Inventory Committee to:
a. Prepare a PIP in coordination with the General Services Office
(GSO) and Accounting Office, and submit the PIP approved by
the Municipal Mayor to the Audit Team at least 10 CD before the
scheduled start of inventory taking activities;
b. Conduct a comprehensive and timely physical inventory of all
PPE in accordance with the approved PIP, with the support of
other concerned offices to ensure completeness and accuracy; and
c. Prepare and submit a properly accomplished RPCPPE based on
the results of the actual physical count, to uphold the integrity of
the agency's property records, in compliance with Section 124 of
the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, not later than
January 31 of each year.

5.14 The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM)
No. 2025-012 dated April 22, 2025 for the foregoing observations and
recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare Report on Local Road Network and conduct Physical Count of the Road Network annually; 

Direct Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Voucher Entry to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card; and	

Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer complete description and cost segregation of road components for road project.
observations.reason: Physical count was not conducted; hence, report was not submitted. The Municipal Accountant was not able to prepare JEV for the local road network. The Municipal Engineer was not able to provide the data for the road networks.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the:
Municipal Treasurer to:
Provide the Municipal Accountant with certified list of RPT Accounts Register/Tax Index as a bases of recording the RPT and SET Receivable and Deferred RPT /Deferred SET Income; and

Intensify the collection of RPT/SET Receivable to include delinquent accounts by providing tax amnesty and post at a conspicuous and publicly accessible place at the municipal hall pursuant to Section 254 of the RA 7160.

Municipal Accountant to:
Establish and record RPT Receivable and SET Receivable, at the beginning of the year, based on the certified list of RPT Accounts Register/Tax Index for the current year provided by the Municipal Treasurer using the following entries:
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer was not able to provide the Municipal Accountant of the certified list of RPTAR/Tax Index. RPT Receivable and SET Receivable was not established and recorded at the beginning of the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments in the books for accounts to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank and other affected accounts; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to return the unutilized balances of fund transfers to the Source Agency as stipulated in the MOA, otherwise, remit the same to the National Treasury.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Maintain the necessary perpetual inventory records in accordance with Section 114 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1;
Record purchases of supplies and materials for stock to their appropriate inventory accounts and recognize as expense only when stocks are issued upon receipt of the SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS as provided in Section 121 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1; and
Reconcile the RPCI after the conduct of periodic physical inventory of supplies or properties to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of Inventory account balances.
Require the GSO designate to:
Consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the SSMI and submit it immediately, along with the original RIS, to the Municipal Accountant as basis of JEV preparation to record the expenditures based on the cost of the supplies and materials issued; and
Conduct a semestral physical count of inventory items by type, prepare the RPCI, and submit to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended and the Management agreed to Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Observe compliance with the perpetual inventory system in the recording of the supplies and materials procured for construction projects implemented by administration by recording all purchases under the account “Construction Materials Inventory”; and
Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer/designate a Municipal GSO who is tasked to implement controls on the receipt and utilization of the same, and to submit a summary of the utilized Construction Materials Inventory monthly to the Accounting Office.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for the unutilized balance of the DRRMF transferred to special trust fund by the year of transfer as provided by Section 5.1.12 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to effectively monitor their specific balances.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to cause the immediate transfer of the unexpended/unobligated QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE amounting to ₱2.993 million to a special trust fund using the following Journal Entries:
For General Fund Books:
Dr.         Subsidy to other Funds                  xx
Cr.         Cash in Bank                                           xx
For Trust Fund Books
Dr.   Cash in Bank                                         xx
Cr     Trust Liability- DRRMF                                    xx
(with SL for the source and year of transfer)

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Development Council to specifically allocate the amount on the formulated projects, instead of lump sum; and
Require the Municipal Engineer to specify in the Program of Work the dimensions and point to point details of the length, its width, and thickness of the concrete work for the expected/accomplished output of the ongoing and future projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit all the lacking documentary requirements as indicated in Annex I;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to check and verify the completeness of supporting documents on various claims before signing; and
Direct all concerned officials/employees to process disbursements vouchers only with complete supporting documents and with the approval of proper/authorized officials to establish its validity/propriety, otherwise, the transaction shall be suspended/ disallowed.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the MPDO to comply with the above provision in the preparation and submission of the approved LDP; and
Instruct the MPDO to regularly monitor and evaluate progress of the PPAs included in the AIP to help identify any deviations from the local budget, plan, and policies, allowing for timely adjustments and better resource utilization.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Prioritize the establishment of the LDRRMO by issuing an ordinance regarding the creation of office; and
Direct the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to require the Human Resource and Management Officer to prioritize the hiring of the three (3) mandatory positions to ensure comprehensive implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management as required in RA No. 10121.

We recommended and the Management agreed to maintain a separate depository account for the special trust fund for LDRRMF in compliance with Section 21, Paragraph 3 of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 92 – 382.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit all the lacking documentary requirements as indicated in Annex K-1;
Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursements should be properly supported with complete documentation and adequate information that can establish the validity of transactions and activities that transpired before signing, and to stop the practice of processing payment for claims that are not sufficiently documented to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspensions or Disallowances; and
Require the drivers to properly accomplish the Driver’s Trip Tickets, and prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption on utilization of fuel, oil and lubricants.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to install signboards in the prescribed project for every infrastructure project; and
Comply with the transparency requirements of the 1987 Constitution as specified in Section 2 of COA Circular 2013-004, in all its infrastructure projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the GSO Designate to comply with the required markings “For Official Use Only” on all motor vehicles of the agency measuring three inches, under which should be written the name “Municipality of Maayon”, to appear on each side, thus, ensuring the exclusive use for official business only; and
Secure government plates for the 20 motor vehicles

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Require all Accountable Officers/Collectors to observe daily remittance of their collections intact as prescribed to lessen the risk of misuse and loss of funds and strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 29 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.;
Direct Municipal Treasurer to monitor the issuance of Accountable Forms to collectors and refrain from issuing additional Official Receipts to collectors unless remittance of collections is made on time; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to secure an official designation for the Liquidating Officer from the Municipal Mayor for appropriate authority to handle cash and proper disposition and accountability.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that Accountable Forms are issued sequentially for monitoring purposes and to properly fill-out data in the RAAF and Report of Collection and Deposits in its issuance for appropriate control;
Direct Liquidating Officer to check the Statement of Accountable Forms (AFs) as to the initial balances on hand, receipts, issuances and to verify the ending balances on hand; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to control issuance of new stubs to Collectors/ Liquidating Officer without sacrificing efficiency.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the Other Payables – Current and Non-current account totaling ₱2.144 million which remained dormant/ unpaid for two years or more to determine its validity.
Revert the Other Payables with no valid claimants to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF pursuant to Section 98 of PD No. 1445; and
Prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers and aging for all Other Payables that would be reverted to the GF for monitoring purpose and future reference in case actual claims would be filed by the respective creditors.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card, exhaust more efforts to identify the details of the PPE with lump sum balances and secure the necessary documents pertaining thereto, if cannot be identified despite of all efforts exerted, disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements the PPE accounts with corresponding lumpsum balances of which specific details cannot be identified by the Management.

Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for all obsolete or unserviceable PPEs which is to be used for dropping from the books.

observations.reason: Certified true copy of the land title donated to the Municipality was not secured.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the Chairman of the BAC to adhere to the provision stated in Item 4.3 of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2020-142 requiring the posting in the GPPB online portal and at any conspicuous place within seven (7) working days from the date of the acceptance of the award in case of direct contracting or negotiated purchase on emergency situation; 

Direct the Municipal Accountant to verify strictly compliance to the standard documentary requirements in every government expenditure transaction; and

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to account for the actual distribution of medical supplies to establish its existence and usage if it attained the objective.

We recommended and Management agreed to: 

a.	Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to identify in the Annual Investment Plan the priority and implementable PPAs that contributes to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and to refrain realignment of approved funded projects; and 

b.	Direct the Municipal Engineer to immediately implement the identified priority PPAs funded from the 20% Development Fund through other modes as deemed necessary.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Limit the hiring of JO personnel based on the necessity and urgency of the department’s needs to avoid redundant, unnecessary services and duplication of work accomplishments; and

Submit accomplishment reports for the work/job performed covering the pay period duly certified by the immediate supervisor.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the MDRRMO to implement PPAs in the Annual Investment Plan for LDRRMF and come up with a supplemental or updated LDRRMIP as necessary to include other activities not originally covered by the appropriation; and

Henceforth, the Municipal Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer should strictly adhere with RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit duly signed list of recipients for the welfare goods distributed valued at P0.508 million procured from LGU LDRRM Funds;  

Direct the Municipal Treasurer/Designated Supply Officer prepare Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE), for Equipment and Inventory Custodian Slips for items not classified as PPEs; and

Instruct the Property and Supply Unit or Municipal Treasurer to record Donated Relief Goods in the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM for each kind of relief goods.

We recommended and Management agreed:

Direct Municipal Rural Health Officer to account for the difference of 254 vials and to secure duly filled-out copies of Requisition and Issue Slips from PHO every time goods are received/transferred from other agencies and reconcile all RIS at year end;

Direct Municipal Rural Health Officer to submit the data to the Municipal Accountant for recording of donated vaccines; 

Direct Municipal Rural Health Officer to prepare and submit Inventory Report of Vaccines as prescribed in Section 6.3 COA Memorandum No. 2021-015 as follows:

dates of receipts and expiration dates.
quantity and cost of vaccines or supplies issued or distributed for inoculation, wastage or spoilage, and remaining balance of vaccines and supplies.

dates of issuance and inoculation.

Direct the Municipal Health Unit to conduct regularly inventory of drugs and medicines to account the number of vaccines received and issued to establish its existence and condition.

We recommended and Management agreed to: 

Review and update the "Chapter 12 - PLAN IMPLEMENTATION" of the LGU's SWM Plan for purposes of ensuring its sustainability, viability, effectiveness, and relevance; 

Enforce strict compliance with the significant provisions of RA No. 9003 and devise local ordinances relative to proper segregation, collection, transport, storage, treatment and disposal of solid waste; and
 
Provide the allocation of funds for the required sanitary landfill if necessary and acquisition of additional SWM equipment; intensify the efforts to ensure a controlled dump site and the implementation of all PPAs relative to the Solid Waste Management Program of the LGU.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
a. Direct the MDRRMO to implement PPAs in the Annual Investment Plan for LDRRMF and
cause the preparation of a supplemental or updated LDRRMIP as necessary to include
other activities not originally covered by the appropriation; and
b. Cause the submission of Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF with
the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD), Department of Interior and Local Government
(DILG) and Department of Budget and Management (DBM).
Henceforth, the Municipal Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer should
strictly adhere with RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Stop charging expenditures to LDRRMF that are not authorized. Submit written
justification on the purpose why administrative and operating expenditures are
charged to LDRRMF instead to Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE)
or Capital Outlay (CO) of the General Fund; and
b. Direct the Municipal Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer /Council
to include only activities that adhere to guidelines on the four (4) aspects as provided
in the preparation and utilization of LDRRMF.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
1. Municipal Accountant to properly recognize inventory as assets following the Perpetual
Inventory Method;
2. Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for Supplies and Materials
delivered; and
3. Supply Officer to immediately prepare and submit to the OIC - Municipal Accountant
the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) together with the original copy of
the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) covering the period January 1 - December 31, 2021
as provided by Section 121 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs to be used by the
Accounting Unit as basis in preparing the JEV to record supplies and materials issued.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to retrieve all available records on the execution of
Deed of Donations;
b. Request a certified true copy of the land title donated to Municipality of Maayon from
Registry of Deeds;
c. Require Municipal Treasurer to keep track of documents relative to Properties of the
Municipality; and
d. Come up with a proposed budget for litigation, titling and relocation and make
representation with the Sangguniang Bayan to prioritize the appropriation of funds
necessary to pursue the claim and transfer of Torrens Title in its name of the
Municipality.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
a. Direct the property custodian/Municipal Treasurer to maintain an efficient property
management by following the prescribed procedures in property recording and
maintain accurate and complete property records;
b. Submit Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property for CY 2021;
c. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to make a representation with the
Local Chief Executive to designate additional person to be in-charge of
recording/posting newly acquired assets and unserviceable PPEs;
d. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the property custodian to reconcile the Property,
Plant and Equipment accounts and adjust the books of accounts and the property
records respectively, as warranted; and
e. Continuously reconcile the result of the physical count/cleansing with the property
and accounting records.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Health Officer and/or concerned RHU personnel to furnished the
Municpal Accountant, the documents necessary to record the reciept and distribution of
vaccines and Inventory Reports or its equivalent in relation to the vaccination program;
and
b. Direct the Municpal Accountant to record using the perpetual inventory system, the
vaccines received and its distribution as reported by the LGU to DOH-CHD and draw
journal entry voucher for the applicable inventory accounts to record the remaining
vaccines in stock at December 31, 2021.

We recommended and the Management agreed to submit distribution sheets duly signed by recipients/household of rice and other assorted food packs as soon as possible, otherwise, corresponding Notice of Suspension/Disallowance will be issued to concerned Offices.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant to approved payroll or list of payees indicating their net pay and to submit certification that previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books before a cash advance is approved.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) within five days from month-end, to facilitate the proper recording of transaction and to ensure validated inventory balances and expenses accounts;
b. Require Municipal Treasurer and other supplies/inventory custodians to undertake the actual physical inventory at least once a year and to submit RPCI to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 each year; and
c. Enjoin GSO/all inventory custodians and Municipal Accountant to perform the reconciliation of the inventories with the balances in the books of accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, GAD Focal Point System and its Technical Working Group to fully establish and maintain an operational GAD Database pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Request for write-off from the books for all dormant accounts,  in case full efforts prove futile, after complying with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and

Make necessary adjusting entries to the accounts affected in case the request for write-off will be granted.

Request for write-off be done after complying the necessary requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, the General Services Officer, and the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Mayor strictly adhere with COA Circular  No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.  Likewise, the Municipal Accountant exert effort in recording the remaining Local Road Networks implemented by the Municipality prior to CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that: 

Municipal Accountant should serve a final written notice and demand settlement to personnel  with unliquidated cash advances aged 3 years and above; 

Request for write-off from the books, in case full efforts to enforce settlement of outstanding Cash Advances prove futile, after complying with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and

Municipal Accountant,  in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, should strictly comply with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in granting and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the OIC Municipal Treasurer/Disbursing Officer submit to COA Office duly verified/accepted accomplishment report from the Punong Barangay where the projects were undertaken/located and the corresponding pictures if any.
	
It is also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan Members and the Vice Mayor discontinue their practice of preparing the POW for Barangay or Municipal projects. The preparation thereof should be the responsibility of the Municipal Engineer.

We recommended the following:

Management advise the Agency Authorized Officer to be more diligent in approving the Loan Applications of employees, and ensure that loan amortizations be deducted monthly without sacrificing the P5,000.00 minimum net take home pay, as required by the law; and

Preparation of monthly payroll for salaries and PERA be centralized at the Accounting Office for easy monitoring, control, and strict implementation of related provisions and restrictions of the law.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Request for write-off from the books for all dormant accounts,  in case full efforts prove futile, after complying the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and

Make necessary adjusting entries to the accounts affected in case the request for write-off will be granted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, the General Services Officer, and the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Mayor strictly adhere with COA Circular  No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.  Likewise, the Municipal Accountant exert effort in recording the remaining Local Road Networks implemented by the Municipality prior to CY 2019.

We recommended the following:

Sanggunian Bayan formulate ordinances to maximize imposition of taxes, fees and other charges, and/or include to offer for tax amnesty thru an ordinance to generate more revenues; 

Municipal Treasurer issue Notice of Delinquencies and Demand Letters as collection method to delinquent taxpayers; and

Enforce collection remedies allowed as provided under RA No. 7160, particularly on the sale of delinquent properties through public auction.
We recommended that Management make sure that the building/structure be utilized solely as Municipal Crisis Center.

Municipal Accountant should serve a final written notice and demand settlement to personnel  with unliquidated cash advances aged 3 years and above; 

Request for write-off from the books, in case full efforts to enforce settlement of outstanding Cash Advances prove futile, after complying with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and

Municipal Accountant,  in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, should strictly comply with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in granting and liquidation of cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
1. Municipal Accountant should serve a final written notice and demand settlement to
personnel with unliquidated cash advances aged 3 years and above;
2. Request for write-off from the books, in case full efforts to enforce settlement of
outstanding Cash Advances prove futile, after complying with the requirements of
COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
3. Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, should strictly
comply with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in granting and liquidation of
cash advances.

We recommended that the OIC Municipal Treasurer/Disbursing Officer submit to COA
Office duly verified/accepted accomplishment report from the Punong Barangay where
the projects were undertaken/located and the corresponding pictures if any.
It is also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan Members and the Vice Mayor
discontinue their practice of preparing the POW for Barangay or Municipal projects. The
preparation thereof should be the responsibility of the Municipal Engineer.

We recommended the following:
a. Management advise the Agency Authorized Officer to be more diligent in
approving the Loan Applications of employees, and ensure that loan
amortizations be deducted monthly without sacrificing the P5,000.00 minimum net
take home pay, as required by the law; and
b. Preparation of monthly payroll for salaries and PERA be centralized at the
Accounting Office for easy monitoring, control, and strict implementation of related
provisions and restrictions of the law.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
1. Request for write-off from the books for all dormant accounts, in case full efforts
prove futile, after complying with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005
dated December 19, 2016; and
2. Make necessary adjusting entries to the accounts affected in case the request for
write-off will be granted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, the General Services Officer, and the
Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Mayor strictly adhere with COA Circular No.
2015-008 dated November 23, 2015. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant exert effort in
recording the remaining Local Road Networks implemented by the Municipality prior to
CY 2019.

We recommended that Management:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger
Cards for each category of assets and Real Property Ledger Cards for land;
2. Require the OIC Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards for each category of
assets;
3. Direct the Inventory/Appraisal Committee to assess the value of the assets submitted
per office/.division and prepare/certify correct the corresponding RPCPPE to be
approved by the Municipal Mayor;
4. Submit the same inventory report to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each
year; and
5. Reconciled RPCPPE with the accounting records and make necessary adjustments
if there are discrepancies noted.

We recommended the following:
a. Sanggunian Bayan formulate ordinances to maximize imposition of taxes,
fees and other charges, and/or include to offer for tax amnesty thru an
ordinance to generate more revenues;
b. Municipal Treasurer issue Notice of Delinquencies and Demand Letters as
collection method to delinquent taxpayers; and
c. Enforce collection remedies allowed as provided under RA No. 7160,
particularly on the sale of delinquent properties through public auction.

We recommended that Management make sure that the building/structure be utilized
solely as Municipal Crisis Center.

We recommended that Management:
1. OIC - Municipal Accountant should serve a final written notice and demand settlement to personnel with unliquidated cash advances aged 3 years and above;
2. Request for write-off from the books, in case full efforts to enforce settlement of outstanding Cash Advances prove futile, after complying with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
3. OIC-Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, should strictly comply with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in granting and cash advances.

a. Republic Act No. 10121 series of 2010;
b. Section A of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012; and
c. DBM-DILG-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014, henceforth, the Municipal Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer, in coordination with other concerned units of the LGU, should strictly adhere with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the preparation of PPAs for LDRRMFIP and its utilization.

b. Submit the same inventory report to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year; and

observations.recommendation: Request for write-off from the books,  in case efforts to enforce settlement of Cash Advances prove futile, after complying with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 (copy of which attached herewith for ready reference).

We recommended the following: Require the ICO – Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payee at least one month before a check become stale, reminding him of the existence of the outstanding check;
Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant: Request for write-off from the books,  in case full efforts prove futile, after complying with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016;
Make necessary adjusting entries to adjust the Accounts affected in case the request for write-off will be granted.

We recommended that Management: Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the required annual physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE and to prepare/certify correct the corresponding RPCPPE to be approved by the Municipal Mayor;
Submit the same inventory report to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year;
Reconcile RPCPPE with the accounting records and make necessary adjustments if there are discrepancies noted.

We recommended that:

    1.  OIC-Municipal Accountant should serve a  final  written  notice  and
       demand settlement      to personnel  with unliquidated cash  advances
       aged 3 years and above;


    2. Request for write-off from the books, in  case  diligent  efforts  to
       enforce settlement of outstanding Cash Advances prove  futile,  after
       complying with the requirements of COA Circular  No.  2016-005  dated
       December 19, 2016; and


    3.  OIC-Municipal  Accountant,  in  coordination  with   the   Municipal
       Treasurer, should strictly comply with the pertinent laws, rules  and
       regulations in granting cash advances.


    The observations and  recommendations  herein  cited  were  covered  by
                         AOM No. 2019-01 (2018) dated January 18, 2019.

We recommended that management plan and implement Project, Program  and
    Activities (PPAs) in the LDRRMF Budget  that  are  compliant  with  the
    following laws, rules and regulations:

        a. Republic Act No. 10121 series of 2010;
        b. Section A of DILG Memorandum Circular No.  2012-73  dated  April
           17, 2012; and
        c. DBM-DILG-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4,
           2014.


    Henceforth, the Municipal Local Disaster Risk Reduction and  Management
    Officer, in coordination with other concerned units of the LGU,  should
    strictly adhere with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations  in  the
    preparation of PPAs for LDRRMFIP and its utilization.

We recommended that Management:


      a. Direct the OIC  –  Municipal  Treasurer  to  prepare  statement  of
        accounts for the Lessees,  and  require  to  pay  the  corresponding
        unpaid monthly rentals and penalty/ies before  any  renewal  of  the
        contract in their favor;


      b. Require  the  OIC-Municipal  Treasurer  to  maintain  complete  and
        updated Subsidiary Ledgers of rental income of the occupants;
      c.  Require  the  OIC-Municipal  Treasurer  to  monitor  strictly  the
         collection of market   rentals; and

      d.   Strictly  enforce  compliance  with  the  terms  and   conditions
         stipulated in the Lease contract.


      The observations and recommendations  herein  cited  were  covered  by
                            AOM No. 2019-03 (2018) dated January 24, 2019.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant:


      a. Request for write-off from the books,   in  case  diligent  efforts
        prove futile, after complying with the requirements of COA  Circular
        No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and


      b. Make necessary adjusting entries to the Accounts affected  in  case
        the request for write-off is granted.

      The observations and recommendations  herein  cited  were  covered  by
                            AOM No. 2019-05(2018) dated February 13, 2018.

We recommended that Management:


    a.   Direct the Inventory Committee to  conduct  per  department/office
       annual  physical   inventory   of   the   Municipality’s   PPE   and
       prepare/certify correct the corresponding RPCPPE to be  approved  by
       the Municipal Mayor;


    b.  Submit the same inventory report to the Audit Team not  later  than
       January 31 of  each year; and


    c.  Reconcile RPCPPE with the accounting  records  and  make  necessary
       adjustments if there are discrepancies noted.


    The observations and  recommendations  herein  cited  were  covered  by
                         AOM No. 2019-04 (2018) dated February 12, 2019.
We recommended that Management: Exert more efforts to recover the unsettled Cash Advances;

OIC Municipal Accountant should conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the unliquidated cash advances and determine the concerned accountable officers and employees;
OIC Municipal Accountant should serve a written notice and demand settlement to personnel with unliquidated cash advances aged 3 years and above;

Require the OIC Municipal Accountant to evaluate outstanding checks aging above six months which are considered stale per Bank Reconciliation Statements as of November 30, 2017 and make necessary adjustments and prepare JEV to record the transactions as Accounts Payable in accordance with Section 59 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, Vol. I;
Maintain a separate Subsidiary Ledger to facilitate processing of future claims.

We recommended that OIC Municipal Accountant ensure that the claims for RATA should be properly reviewed and are supported with complete documentation before certifying and effecting payment in order to avoid suspension and /or disallowance of the transactions
We recommended the following: Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, allowances, fringe benefits and other emoluments through bank;
Make representation with any government bank for an opening of individual ATM savings accounts by employees through a Memorandum of Agreement;
We recommended that: LDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within 15 days after the end of each month using the format hereto attached; and
Make representation with the Municipal Mayor to request for additional personnel for the Office of the LDRRM or to assign personnel who will regularly prepare the required report.

We recommended that Management: Submit copies of the MOA/ Trust Agreement for the various programs/projects the Municipality had implemented thru fund transfers, otherwise the subject transaction shall be suspended/disallowed in audit;
Enhance review mechanism on processing of disbursement vouchers;
Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 3.1 and 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Section 4(3) of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that Management immediately stop the utilization of SEF for purposes other than those provided in Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 issued by DepEd, DBM and DILG and Section 272 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the Focal Person should prepare and furnish the Audit Team the accomplishment reports for the utilization and implementation of the eleven PPAs for the Municipal Council for the Protection of Children.
Henceforth, the periodic preparation of such report should be observed.

We recommended that Management should re-organize/update the members of the Municipality’s SWMB to include only officials/employees in active service and to designate a focal person in-charge of the SWM program/activities of the Municipality pursuant to Section 12 of R.A. No. Act 9003. It is also recommended that Management fast track the preparation and submission of the 10- Year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan to the Provincial Solid Waste Management Board for the latter to collate the data and to be submitted to the Commission for approval.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point Person: Make representation with the Local Chief Executive for the latter to require the GAD projects implementor to submit their respective accomplishment reports within the set timelines;
Furnish immediately a copy of the GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team for monitoring purposes and performance evaluation of GAD-related projects/programs and activities; and
Henceforth, the Agency should comply with COA Circular 2014-01 in relation to its GAD programs, projects and activities.

We recommended that Management: Establish the existence, condition, validity and accuracy of its fixed assets by conducting a physical inventory at least once a year in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and Expedite the inventory-taking in accordance with the following prescribed procedures: Creation of Inventory Committee A committee shall be formed to take charge of the physical inventory-taking, consisting of two or more employees designated by the head of the agency including the property/administrative officer or custodian depending on the extent of his accountability. Property Identification and Coding Government property shall be properly labeled and identification by the Inventory Committee. Inventory labels of special materials shall contain among others the individual property number to be provided by the corporation concerned. These numbers shall be uniformly printed in India ink, to achieve permanence and best results, and shall be posted on prominent but secure portion of the property for easy identification. Acknowledgment Receipt for equipment (ARE) An officer or employee who receives and is in possession or physical control of the property shall be issued an ARE. Such ARE shall be renewed every three (3) years or every time there is a change in accountability as required by NGAS, Volume II. Inventory Reports The report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment shall be prepared on the prescribed format by the NGAS, Volume II. The report shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year for the property, plant and equipment.

Management: Require the immediate liquidation/settlement by concerned officials/employees of all cash advances totaling ₱445,863.04; Cause the withholding of salaries of officials and employees who are still in active service, effective immediately, for unliquidated cash advances totaling ₱230,433.00 granted in prior years; Require the liquidation/settlement of cash advances granted to personnel who are planning to retire/resign; Henceforth, strictly comply with COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and E.O No. 298 dated March 23, 2004 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances and Item 6.0 of COA Cir. 2012-004 dated Nov. 28, 2012 on the Demand for Immediate Liquidation and Settlement of All Cash Advances Outstanding as of December 31, 2011.

We recommended that Management Require the Accounting Office to cause the recording of all prior year’s Public Infrastructures in the RPI as prescribed under Section 23 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II for reference and as basis in the recognition of public infrastructure projects as part of PPE in the Municipality’s books as required under Section 3.7 of COA Circular No. 2016-004, dated September 30, 2016.

The following: Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, allowances, fringe benefits and other emoluments through bank; Make a representation with any government bank for an opening of individual ATM savings accounts by employees through a Memorandum of Agreement; and Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended that Management re-organize/update the members of the Municipality’s SWMB to include only officials/employees in active service and to designate a focal person in-charge of the SWM program/activities of the Municipality pursuant to Section 12 of R.A. No. Act 9003.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management establish a MRF in every barangay or cluster of barangays to ensure the attainment of the goals and objectives of the Solid Waste Management Act.
We recommended that Management clearly specify in the LDRRMFIP the starting and completion dates of the PPAs identified for implementation during the year.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management: Immediately stop the utilization of SEF for purposes other than those provided in the Letter of Instruction No. 1462, series of 1985, implementing guidelines of RA 5447; Sections 100 and 272 of R.A. No. 7160; DECS-DBM-DILG nos. 01, series of 1998, supplemented and clarified per DECS-DBM-DILG nos. 01-A, series of 2000 and 01-B, series of 2001 and Section 83 of the Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units, Volume I; and Instruct the members of the Local School Board to formulate PPAs in accordance with the above stated provisions of law.

In view thereof, we recommended that Management submit copies of the MOA/ Trust Agreement for the various programs/projects the Municipality had implemented thru fund transfers to support transactions/disbursements pertaining thereto and for the evaluation and validation of these projects. Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 3.1 and 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Section 4(3) of P.D. No. 1445.

    a. Exert more efforts to recover the unsettled Cash Advances;


    b.  OIC  Municipal  Accountant  should  conduct  regular  and  periodic
       verification, analysis, and  validation  of  the  existence  of  the
       unliquidated cash advances and determine the  concerned  accountable
       officers and employees;


    c.    OIC Municipal Accountant should serve a written notice and demand
       settlement
       to personnel  with unliquidated  cash  advances  aged  3  years  and

       (copy of which attached herewith for ready reference).

       individual ATM savings accounts by employees through a Memorandum of
       Agreement; and


    c. Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of  the  Memorandum
       of Agreement.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant:

    a. Request for write-off from the books,  in case full  efforts prove
       futile, after complying with the requirements of  COA  Circular  No.
       2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and


    b. Make necessary adjusting entries to adjust the Accounts affected  in
       case the request for write-off will be granted.

a. Direct the  Inventory  Committee  to  conduct  the  required  annual
       physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE and to  prepare/certify
       correct the corresponding RPCPPE to be  approved  by  the  Municipal
       Mayor;


    b. Submit the same inventory report to the Audit Team  not  later  than
       January 31 of each year; and


    c. Reconcile RPCPPE with the  accounting  records  and  make  necessary
       adjustments if there are discrepancies noted.

We recommended for strict compliance with Paragraphs 3-6 of the PPSAS 17 and Sections 2 to 5 of COA Circular No. 2003-007 dated December 11, 2003. For reference, the Table of Estimated Useful Life of PPE is hereto attached as Annex H.

Management ensure that prescribe controls over cash advances be observed. Henceforth, strictly comply with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

Require the concerned RHU personnel to refund the amount of excess claim totaling ₱87,750.00 otherwise a Notice of Disallowance will be issued; Require the Accounting Department to check the correctness of the claims for Subsistence Allowance, ensure the completeness of DTRs attached to the payroll and to see to it that payments for allowances and other personnel benefits should be in consonance with laws, rules and regulations authorizing such payments to avoid suspensions/disallowances in audit; Require the Disbursing Officer to make a cash advance for Subsistence Allowance only after the end of each month and to pay only those employees with DTRs attached to the payroll; and Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 8 of DBM and JC No. 1, series of 2012 dated November 29, 2012 on the Rules and Regulations on the Grant of Compensation-Related Magna Carta Benefits to PHWs.

We recommended that Management follow strictly the regulations set forth in the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Moreover, the OIC-Municipal Accountant should ensure that all purchases of goods and services using the alternative modes of procurement have the corresponding BAC resolution recommending/justifying to the HOPE the use of an alternative method of procurement and duly approved by the HOPE.

Require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to prepare the certified list of tax payers with the amounts due and collectible and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant; and Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to establish/draw a JEV to record in the Municipality’s books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Tax accounts as required in Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Vol. I.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management submit a copy of the Contracts/Purchase Orders irrespective of amount, and the corresponding supporting documents to the Audit Team, within five (5) working days from perfection/issuance thereof, in compliance with COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant: Submit immediately the accounts and reports listed in Annex K which are already overdue; and Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 70 and 73 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Vol. 1 and Section 6.05 of COA Circular 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 on the timely submission of reports and records of receipts and disbursements to facilitate auditorial review.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Designated Special Disbursing Officer and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer update their cashbooks and balance daily the Cash-Local Treasury, Cash-In-Bank and Cash Advances cashbooks to establish cash accountability at the end of the month and observe consistently the ruling, footing and certifying of the cashbooks to provide accounting control in conformity with Sections 26, 27 and 28 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that Management require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to stamp “PAID” on the face of all paid disbursement vouchers including all attached supporting documents in compliance with COA Circular No. 92-389 dated November 3, 1992 to avoid double payment of claims/expenses.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive, together with the MSWMB strictly implement and comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 on the establishment of a sanitary landfill.

Management: Observe prudence and economy in spending Municipal funds and see to it that the incurrence of expenditures should be dictated by the demands of good government, and that the mission and thrusts of the Municipality incurring the expenditure must be considered in determining whether or not the expenditure is necessary; Require the Accounting Department to submit the liquidation/settlement of cash advances for travelling expenses; Require the DO to submit the liquidation/settlement for her cash advances totaling ₱451,200.00 for the expenses incurred for the conduct of training/seminar such as payment for the venue, payment for speakers/lecturers, and other necessary expenses; and Follow strictly the provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 in the procurement of goods, supplies and materials. Cash advance should only be made for operating expenditures when it is impractical to pay the same by check otherwise, direct payment should be made to suppliers.

We recommended that Management: Submit to the Audit Team the documentary requirements as required under Section 2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for evaluation and review ; and Henceforth, require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the aforementioned law and regulations in releasing government funds to League of Municipalities prior to the approval of the Municipal Mayor or Municipal Vice Mayor, as the case maybe.

Direct the OIC- Municipal Treasurer/ Municipal General Services Officer to prepare RPCPPE in prescribed format in accordance with Sec. 57 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Sector (LGUs) Volume II, to be categorized by type; Direct the OIC- Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and OIC- Municipal Treasurer/GSO to maintain PPE Property Cards to facilitate reconciliation of the PPE accounts; and Henceforth, direct the OIC- Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to reconcile the PPE Ledger Cards as against the RPCPPE and Property Cards.

Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the certified list of tax payers with the amounts due and collectible and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant; and Direct the Municipal Accountant to establish/draw a JEV to record in the Municipality’s books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income accounts as required in Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Vol. I.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to establish the validity, accuracy and completeness of Due to GOCCs (NHA) and Due to NGAs (PSF). Secure copy of the MOA with the National Housing Authority to determine the terms and conditions; Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile the outstanding loans payable balances and that of the records of NHA and Office of the President; Enforce repayments of principal and interest from the NHA housing recipients; Institute collection measures and provide legal sanctions for the non-payment of due and delinquent accounts from the NHA recipients, and Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Ledger according to particular purpose of loan for which it was granted and/or per loan recipient/grantee.

Require the OIC- Municipal Treasurer to immediately prepare and submit to the OIC - Municipal Accountant the SSMI covering the period from January – December 2015; Require the OIC- Municipal Accountant to record in an appropriate asset Inventory Account under the Perpetual Inventory Method all purchases of supplies and materials for stocks as provided in Section 51 of the Manual of NGAS for LGUs Volume I and pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units. Cause the timely submission of the SSMI to the OIC- Municipal Accountant within five days from month-end, to facilitate the proper recording of stocks issued and to ensure the validity of inventory balances and expenses accounts; and Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 43 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs Volume II, on the preparation of the SSMI by the Municipal Treasurer, which shall be used by the Accounting Unit as basis in preparing the JEV to record supplies and materials issued.

We recommended that Management:


   a. Establish the existence, condition, validity and accuracy of its fixed
      assets by conducting a physical inventory at  least  once  a  year  in
      compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume  I;
      and


   b.  Expedite  the  inventory-taking  in  accordance  with  the  following
      prescribed procedures:


     i. Creation of Inventory Committee


        A committee shall be formed to take charge of the physical inventory-
        taking, consisting of two or more employees designated by  the  head
        of the  agency  including  the  property/administrative  officer  or
        custodian depending on the extent of his accountability.


    ii. Property Identification and Coding


        Government property shall be properly labeled and identification  by
        the Inventory Committee. Inventory labels of special materials shall
        contain among others the individual property number to  be  provided
        by the corporation  concerned.  These  numbers  shall  be  uniformly
        printed in India ink, to achieve permanence and  best  results,  and
        shall be posted on prominent but secure portion of the property  for
        easy identification.


   iii. Acknowledgment Receipt for equipment (ARE)


        An officer or employee who receives and is in possession or physical
        control of the property shall be issued an ARE. Such  ARE  shall  be
        renewed every three (3) years or every time there  is  a  change  in
        accountability as required by NGAS, Volume II.


    iv. Inventory Reports


        The report on the physical count of property,  plant  and  equipment
        shall be prepared on the prescribed format by the NGAS,  Volume  II.
        The report shall be submitted to the  Auditor  concerned  not  later
        than January 31 of each year for the property, plant and equipment.


   c. Cause the reconciliation of the inventory reports with the  accounting
      and property records.


    The observations and recommendations herein cited were  covered  by  AOM
              No. 2017-009 (2016) dated February 20, 2017.

Allocate at least 5 percent of the total agency’s budget appropriations to correspond to activities supporting GAD Programs, Activities and Plans (PAPs) as provided in A.1 Section 6.1 of PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No.2012-01 and of COA Circular 2014-001 dated March 14, 2014 of GAD Fund and activities in government agencies; Spearhead the preparation of the annual and performance-based LGU GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) in response to the gender issues and concerns of their locality and in the context of LGU mandate. Once the budget and program adjustments have been made on final agency budget, agency should submit an adjusted GPB to PCW/ DILG. The adjusted GPB shall be the basis for implementing the GPBs as well as for monitoring and reporting; Consider  only expenses that can be charged to GAD budget as provided in Column 7; GAD Budget, Annex A of PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular 2012-01; Give priority to emergency and/or continuing issues and concerns in identifying GAD PAPs as provided in Section 6.4 of PCW-DILG-DBM, NEDA and Joint Circular No. 2013-01 and Magna Carta for Women; and Formulate and pass GAD Codes or ordinance to support LGU’s effort in promoting, protecting and fulfilling women’s rights, women’s economic empowerment and gender-responsive governance in accordance with GAD Code Guidelines of the PCW.

Include priority program projects and activities in the annual school board budget in accordance with the provisions of the Letter of Instruction No. 1462, series of 1985, Implementing Guidelines of RA 5447 and other similar succeeding issuances; and Direct the Budget Officer and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to charge to SEF only priority PPAs as contained in the approved annual school board budget.

Strictly comply with the provision of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 Guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% component of the Annual Internal Revenue Allotment for Development Projects; Cause the immediate identification/revision of the AIP to include the sufficient and specific description and details of projects to be implemented with LGU counterpart for special project; Submit justification why medical supplies and diesel fuel used for garbage collection were charged to 20% Development Fund; and Submit justification why some projects were implemented that were not originally identified in the CY 2015 approved AIP.

Require the immediate liquidation/settlement by concerned officials/employees of all cash advances totaling P489,101.00 as of December 31, 2015; Cause the withholding of salaries of officials and employees who are still active in service, effective immediately, for unliquidated cash advances totaling P338,750.00 granted in prior years; and Henceforth, strictly comply with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 and Paragraph 3.1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 96-004 dated April 19, 1996, and Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommend that Management direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with Section 04(o) of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and COA Circular No. 2003-07 dated December 11, 2003. For reference, the Table of Estimated Useful Life of PPE is hereto attached as Annex I.
